I'm copying and pasting the review I used at Home Depot, because that is where I bought this compactor. However, I spent a lot of time on this review, and I love buying stuff on Amazon too, so I decided to also put my review here. The exact model I have is the black 15" Elite model, but I'm sure this review applies to just about any of their 15" elite models.

My unit arrived slightly defective. The hold feature doesn't work. So far, I've had a repair guy out three times, and he still has a part on order to try to fix it. Fortunately, the compactor works in normal mode, so this hasn't prevented me from using it. (One other switch broke within the first week, but the repair man was able to fix this on his second visit. So it was only out of commission for about a week.)

The good news is that Broan has been very nice and helpful, and has paid for all of the service visits. So, you can have confidence in their 1-year warranty, and if you get unlucky like me at least you don't have to pay for repairs. With any luck, yours will simply work right out of the box!

Because of these warranty issues I've had to deal with, I can't give this a 5-star review. If it arrived in perfect working order, I would have been happy to give it 5-stars.

But I can still confidently give it 4-stars. I love this compactor. I'll list the positives, and then address

top_profane.text:
I have over the last 20 years owned 4 trash compactors, one Whirlpool and two Sears models, and they they might have been made by Whirlpool anyhow. The compactor is built in under our center isle. When our last Sears model broke, I wanted to see what was available that might be higher quality and perhaps higher capacity. I was not unhappy with the three models I had before, and the pickins are slim in this category. But I found the Broan and after reading the reviews, decided to take the plunge. Here is what I found:

1. You must have a garbage disposal in you sink to deal with organic trash. The house had one when we bought it, and honestly, it is one of the wondrous inventions of the 21st century! All organic trash goes down the drain, so that you are only throwing away paper and plastic essentially, and should never have an issue with odors, although the Broan seems to have an effective odor control feature. Don't really know because out garbage never has a chance to smell.
2. The capacity of this compactor is somewhere near twice the capacity of the other models. This means two things. 1. Fewer trips to the outside garbage can. Fewer bags used. Because of the generous capacity, the unit does not really need or seem to be compacting unless a tremendous amount of trash is in it. We empty the bag once a week because we have once a week garbage pick-up. Honestly,
top_profane.title: Best I've owned...one small complaint

top_profane.text: As my first trash compactor ever I think this is perfect.  I've reduced my weekly trash output roughly 1/3, by compacting the trash.  Instead of paying more to the City for an additional trash bin, I'm able to fit all our weekly trash in the single bin.  After using it a month I wish it had more horsepower, so I could further reduce the trash, but its adequate for my purposes:  smashes most everything into neat compact rectangles that now fit nicely within the trash bin that goes to the curb.  I even have some extra space to start working away on the clutter.  I use it with the Whirlpool trash compactor bags which have held up nicely.  Its not too noisey and seems to be made to withstand our frequent open/shut routine of throwing things away almost hourly.  I would recommend this for "normal" household trash use, which is all I use it for.  Based on my experience thus far  I'm not sure it could handle "shop" trash .
top_profane.title: LOVE IT!

top_profane.text: Stay away from this fridge.  With all of the problems I have had, I will NEVER buy Whirpool family of products ever again.  Read reviews, and issues before even considering this brand.  It only lasted 2 years before problems started happening.  First to go was the ice maker.  I called a repairman out as the warranty had of course just ended.  He said he could replace it for min. ~$300, but said he couldn't guarantee it would last.  Then the deli drawer plastic hinge broke. That's just crappy design, plastic hinges?! Crap.  Lastly is the constant leaking.  The freezer leaks about 12-16 ozs of water all at once every few days.  I researched this, and it is common problem, basically the condensing unit's drain freezes, causing excess water from condensation to not drain into the drain pan where it should evaporate, but buildup in the bottom of the freezer which creates an ice damn, and then spills on your floor.  Piece of crap, stay away

After bad experiences with K crap I was reluctant to buy another coffee maker that uses K cups. I found the Cuisinart SS-10 at a great price compared to K craps price and decided to give it a try. Apparently the Cuisinart uses early K crap technology technology. This maker is to much simpler to use and maintain, Compared to my $170 K crap one that only lasted about a year of light use. The k cup holder on the SS-10 is a single piece (cup, with needle on bottom) that simply pops out for cleaning. The SS-10 is packed with features similar to high end units. Lots of cup sizes to choose from, a digital clock that is accurate, rinse cycle and temp settings to name a few. My preference is 8oz size and xtra hot . Temp in my cup is approx 182° !! Nice and hot. You can set auto on/ off times to. On the side is a hidden compartment that houses a high quality reusable k cup for using your own grounds.
In closing, my opinion on reliability: Cuisinart makes quality products, the the simpler (older) technology the ss-10 uses gives me the feeling that this maker will last many years.

top_rant.text:
UPDATE: Thursday, December 12th, 2013. I've been using my Speed Queen ATE50 washer Dryer combo for over a year now and all I can report is trouble free use, clean clothes and dependability. I started having slight mold issues but Borax reduced it. Using bleach to rinse out the washer once a month cut the mold issue way down. Finally I have it set so only hot water goes into the machine so no matter what the waters settings say hot water is being used all the time. Lucky for me I don't have dress clothes and delicates. My underwear are already gray in color because that's the only color I buy. I found using nothing but HOT WATER in all cycles really reduces the mold issue to almost zero when used with the other steps I outline. Yes I know my solutions are not for most people. Goodness knows I am for sure not most people. But after just over a years use my Speed Queen still works like a champ. No issues at all none nada. Oh and by the way the dryer is still working like gang busters too! I'll take my Speed Queen over the junk it replaced any day.

===================

Rating this three stars because the device gets out some but not all of the dirt, doesn't seem like it's built to last, and has many parts don't even fit together correctly. Arguably, the axle is the most mechanically important component of the washer, since that's the mechanism by which the whole thing works, but, unfortunately, the axle could not be securely positioned on my unit due to poorly constructed fittings. Also, if it matters to you, the wonder wash is, indeed, pink.

Oh, and, this doesn't affect my rating, but if you were considering using this in an apartment, don't just think about the money you might save. As far as induction goes and as far as size, portability, and power goes, this device is adequate and reasonably priced for its power output and ease of use.

The induction stovetop does have problem controlling temperature like some other reviewer have said. This is especially problematic for low settings at 1, 2, and 3. Now, I want to make sure you understand the difference between power setting and temperature setting. There is no such thing as "temperature setting" preset because the temperature is completely dependent of the size of the pot, the amount of ferrous material used, the thickness of the pot, and the amount of water/food in the pot. To set a temperature without knowing these variables is simply impossible. The stove guesses it by assigning a guessed power level to heat the pot. Ok, so know we understand. The only remaining variable to play with is the Power level ranging from 1-10. The stove does not exactly have 10 different power levels -- instead, at power 1, 2, and 3 the stove switches on and off at different intervals. This is like your microwave oven. The 50% setting, for example, is where your microwave turns on and off 50% of the time. The microwave does not have a "half power" microwave emission. So, imaging you turning your gas stove on and off 50% of the time, this is how Induction mimics 50% power setting. Does

By the time I got to the fridge I was SOAKED IN SWEAT...one day I just snapped and said THATS IT...I NEED A MINI WINE FRIDGE & MINI ICEMAKER IN THE LIVINGROOM!!!!

BEST IDEA EVER!!!! I keep 2 filtered (Brita Water) bottles of water in my mini wine fridge so I don't have to walk to the kitchen each time it needs to be filled....also I noticed really cold water tends to make it run longer...normal time for large ice cubes is 12 minutes with water around 50 degrees (about 115-120 minutes for the entire bucket to be filled)...it takes much longer for ice cubes to be made if the water is colder. The ice stays a VERY LONG TIME inside the chamber/ice bucket once you get the red/ice full light!!! What's even better is when the ice cubes melt...the water is being recycled back into the icemaker!!! So you really don't have to refill it as much if you think about it😊😊😊😊

#2 Icemaker has just been turn

This is a Wonderfully Amazing Machine!!! This is NOT a flimsy little machine. It is a heavy duty ice maker!!! It makes so much ice that I have to turn it off at times because my freezer is small and it gets full quickly!!! This machine has an off and on switch, so you can simply turn it off when you don't need it or when you have enough ice. The small ice cube setting makes a very large ice cube!!! It comes with a nice ice scoop that I keep in a gallon freezer bag in my freezer to dump my fresh ice into so that it will freeze hard. For those complaining about how wet the ice is when it comes out, the ice I buy in bags is wet, too!!! ICE IS WET before it freezes!!! Just put your fresh ice in a baggie, allow it to freeze hard, then break it up by slamming it on your counter, and dump it into your ice bag. At least, you won't have a huge lump of ice cubes all frozen together!!!

This machine is heavy, so take it out of the box close to the table or counter where you plan to use it. You'll want to allow it to sit for 2 hours before using it because the Freon (or coolant) has to settle in the machine. The cleaning instructions are confusing, so I just wiped it down good and filled the reservoir. I threw out all the ice that it made out, drained it, and sopped up all the water, then refilled it. It makes ice very quickly, so soon I was putting ice in the freez
top_exclaim.title: This is a BIG ICE MAKER and I am so happy that I bought it!!!  No more lugging home bags of ice!!!

Old one was almond painted, and although it still worked well, it was starting to really look like crap, with paint worn off the edges and corners. Also hooks up the same was as the old one.  Surprisingly (given that some of the reviews complain about the noise), it is much quieter than the old one.  Also, of course (since it's new) it looks a whole hell of a lot better.  Otherwise, it's pretty much about the same as the old one.  If this one lasts 26 years, it will outlast my wife and myself. top_five_star_obscene.text:
When I came across this I immediately thought “how have I never thought of this?” I used to lug around my French press on travel (because I am a coffee snob and crap like Starbucks ain’t gonna cut it). This is such a light and portable way to have great coffee. It is like a cross between pour over and French press. It is also so easy to clean that I’ve found myself using it more often than any of my other brewing methods even at home. Hate putting wet coffee grounds in the trash because it makes the trash rot quicker? Leave the grounds sitting in this thing until they air dry and then dump them in the trash. The flavor is great because it doesn’t capture the oils and leave filter flavor like a paper filter does and although it leaves some sediment in the bottom (which I actually prefer) it captures more than any French press I’ve owned. If you’re getting too much sediment, adjust your grind size. Seriously I didn’t know I could love a tiny piece of plastic this much.

My one concern is that it is made of plastic and I am afraid it could be leaching some as you pour hot water into it. However I’ve had this thing for probably a year or so and it’s seen daily use and hasn’t shown any signs of wear and I never taste any flavors other than coffee so if it is leaching plastic, it’s probably not any more plastic than you’re getting straight from the tap.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Simple, yet effective

1. Throw in the trash the screws and crappy anchors that come with the unit and buy #10 screws with a hex head that you can use a socket on with your drill. So much easier to install this way and you won't risk stripping out the head of your screw in these awkward positions.
2. Temporarily mount a 2x4 on the wall about a half inch below the lower edge of the hood. This gives you the ability to place the hood up there and mark the locations of where the screws go in. I was able to install it myself without a helper (but a helper is nice to have). Pre-drill your screw holes. When you're ready to mount the hood, install two screws where the keyhole slots are in the hood, but leave them sticking out about 3/8". This will allow you to lift the hood up onto the 2x4 and then wiggle the hood over the screw heads, and slide it back into the narrow end of the keyhole slot. Tighten those two screws. Then install the other other two screws that have a plain hole in the hood top.

top_five_star_obscene.text:
When I bought my Keurig at Costco about 5 years ago it came with this refillable big gray cup. I had to remove the black piece with the pin to make it work. It was 3 pieces and a little annoying to clean. After using that for a couple months I went to strictly regular K-cups. I had no idea a product like this was even on the market. After using it a bit here’s what I think:

• No hassle: This is almost as easy as throwing a regular K-cup in. I just fill it up to the top of the mesh. Align it where the poking pin fits in the groove in the bottom of the purple cup, which is very easy. Put the handle down and I’m ready to go. I don’t have to take the black piece out of the Keurig. I didn't find a single coffee ground in my cup of coffee.
• Cleaning: To clean I shake out the grinds in the garbage and put the cup on a peg in the top of the dishwasher.
• Construction: I think these are made pretty well, but they are a thin plastic that I’m guessing will withstand about 3-6 months of use. I will update when they start breaking. Given that they are about $6 a piece the quality is on par for that price. The lid takes a little pressure to get it all the way closed.
• Value: I buy Costco K cups and this product is about what I pay for 50 cups (100 of them is about $33). I know out of these three cups I’ll get a hundreds of cups of coffee. I only buy Costco K-cups
top_five_star_obscene.title: These are very easy to use and great for the environment. Much better than the reusable one I got years ago.

top_five_star_obscene.text:
We chose this and its dryer mate because the NY Times reviewed it as best for the year model for cleaning cloths, gentleness on cloths, and expected durability and longevity of the product line. This is a DAY1 review.

First the Bad:

1. the manuals are terrible. There is so much missing about how to use the controls it is pitiful. Consider for example that there is NOTHING in the dryer manual to tell you how to use all the potential button settings. None of the buttons worked, until I figured out hours later that you must use the MANUAL selection on the dial before you can change stuff.. If you go to the LG web site, there are other documents you can read but I mean really. As a Software Engineer, if my QA and DOCUMENTATION people wrote manuals this bad about what I build, I would fire them.

2. availability is spotty on both the appliances and their accessories. We had trouble finding the matching dryer, and could not get the pedestal add ones because none were available. Chalk this up to COVID I guess but one outfit said we would need to wait five weeks to the matching dryer. So do some research and shop around to see who has what in stock. If your current washer/dryer are on their last leg, maybe don't wait for them to fail before you but new ones? Just a thought.

3. the washer is louder that both the wife and I expected.
top_five_star_obscene.title: ONLY a First Day review... all is good though.
